summary: This topic describes the process of assigning optional subjects to individual students, differentiating it from general subjects which are assigned to all students in a class.
navigation: Subject Class Mapping (ensure subject is optional)
steps: Ensure the subject is defined as 'Optional' in 'Subject Class Mapping'.
Navigate to the 'Assigned Subject' option (or similar icon/menu item).
Select the 'Class' for which you want to assign optional subjects.
A list of students in that class will be displayed.
Select the specific 'Students' who will be taking the optional subject.
Select the 'Optional Subject' from the list.
Add the subject to assign it to the selected students.
business_rules: Optional subjects are not automatically assigned to all students in a class.
General subjects can be assigned to all students in a class using a bulk assignment option.
important_notes: If a subject is mapped as 'General', there is an option to assign it to all students in bulk directly from the mapping screen or a dedicated bulk assignment feature.
questions: How do I assign an optional subject to specific students?
What is the process for assigning optional subjects to students?
Can I assign a general subject to all students in a class at once?
Where do I define a subject as optional?
What is the difference in assignment process for general vs. optional subjects?
